issue_title: ES|QL: empty patterns in DISSECT command return empty attribute name and null value
issue_body:
```
ROW a="b c d"
| DISSECT a "%{b} %{} %{d}"
```
returns

```
       a       |       b       |               |       d       
---------------+---------------+---------------+---------------
b c d          |b              |null           |d 
```

it should ignore the empty pattern instead, and only return `a, b, d`
commit_message:
Fix DISSECT with empty patterns (#102580)

Fixes https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/102577

Ignore empty DISSECT patterns, eg. `DISSECT a "%{a} %{} %{b}"` will only
return two fields (`a` and `b`) and will ignore the empty pattern
(discarding the corresponding values).

issue_title: ESQL: Enrich performance degradation twice in last month
issue_body: Twice in the last month the performance of `ENRICH` command has dropped dramatically: * On the 15th November, single enrich dropped from 50ms to 85ms * On the 24th November, it dropped further to 100ms See https://elasticsearch-benchmarks.elastic.co/#tracks/esql/nightly/default/30d On the 15th the build run was `2128866bf1022046877ea255016dfa0feccb0c30`, and on the 24th it was `b12b38ca46fbc50a664d7389d6ff417e9485d8e7`.
commit_message:
Fast path for reading single doc with ordinals (#102902)

This optimization is added for enrich lookups, which are likely to match 
a single document. The change decreases the latency of the enrich
operation in the nyc_taxis benchmark from 100ms to 70ms. When combined
with #102901, it further reduces the latency to below 40ms, better than
the previous performance before the regression.

Relates #102625

commit_message:
Address test issues in TermsTests#testSupportsConcurrentExecution (#102658)

The cardinality function needs to return the same value from both calls, or
it ends up comparing two different scenarios.

Closes #102652

issue_body:
The failure indicates a leaked multi-search response. I took a quick look and see several usages of `multiSearch` in the ML codebase which do not appear to close their responses. Not sure which one caused this but I guess they all need fixing. Relates #102604.

**Build scan:**
https://gradle-enterprise.elastic.co/s/3viumbl72hyti/tests/:x-pack:plugin:ml:internalClusterTest/org.elasticsearch.xpack.ml.integration.BasicDistributedJobsIT/testMaxConcurrentJobAllocations

commit_message:
Ref count search response bytes (#103763)

Final step in  #102030 ... actually makes `SearchHit` read a releasable bytes reference.
Does still fallback to copying to unrolled buffers here and there which can be removed in follow-ups where it's worth the effort (aggs being the most important one probably).

Hard to create very reliable benchmarks for this because all our macro-benchmarks are quite noisy. Running http logs and PMC though, there's a statistically significant reduction in GC and reduced tail latencies in most benchmarks.

The overhead for ref-counting these bytes isn't visible in profiling as far as I can tell and for large source values, no corresponding large `byte[]` are created any longer outside of the few remaining spots where we copy to pooled buffers.

closes #102657
closes #102030

commit_message:
Fix memory tracking in TopN.Row (#102831)

This commit addresses the issue of missing memory tracking for the 
BitSet in TopN.Row. Instead of introducing BreakingBitSet, we replace
the BitSet with a smaller array of offsets in this PR. Nik suggested to
remove that BitSet, but I haven't looked into that option yet.

Closes #100640
Closes #102683
Closes #102790
Closes #102784

commit_message:
Fix :plugins:repository-hdfs:forbiddenApisJavaRestTest (#102983)

Reworking forbiddenApis check to use gradle worker api exposed a bug in
how we resolve krb5kdc keytab information. This fixes the depenendency to krb5kdc keytab configuration and
its builtBy task.

This also changes the usage of krb5kdc keytab files to be passed directly to task classpath as
they are only required at runtime and directly having them as part of javaRestTestRuntimeOnly would mean precommit
requires krb5kdc compose up which we definitely not want

issue_title: Unable to sort in 8.4+ if a field doesn't exist.
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

8.10.3

### Installed Plugins

_No response_

### Java Version

_bundled_

### OS Version

docker

### Problem Description

Cannot sort by field if there are blank values
{
  "name": "es-1-master-2",
  "cluster_name": "es-1",
  "cluster_uuid": "kQ8-xy6FQeqjR8Zmc_a9HQ",
  "version": {
    "number": "8.10.3",
    "build_flavor": "default",
    "build_type": "docker",
    "build_hash": "c63272efed16b5a1c25f3ce500715b7fddf9a9fb",
    "build_date": "2023-10-05T10:15:55.152563867Z",
    "build_snapshot": false,
    "lucene_version": "9.7.0",
    "minimum_wire_compatibility_version": "7.17.0",
    "minimum_index_compatibility_version": "7.0.0"
  },
  "tagline": "You Know, for Search"
}

### Steps to Reproduce

Steps to recreate issue.
From kibana add sample data sets ['Sample flight data','Sample eCommerce orders']
Create dataview
name:test
index patter:kibana_*
timefield: --- I don't want to use the time filter ---

From discover sort by AvgTicketPrice, or run query
```
POST kibana_*/_search
{
    "sort": [
        {
            "AvgTicketPrice": {
                "order": "desc",
                "unmapped_type": "boolean"
            }
        }
    ],
    "fields": [
        {
            "field": "*",
            "include_unmapped": "true"
        }
    ],
    "size": 500
}
```
Can't sort on field [AvgTicketPrice]; the field has incompatible sort types: [LONG] and [FLOAT] across shards!

### Logs (if relevant)

_No response_
commit_message:
Allow mismatched sort-by field types if there are no docs to sort (#102779)

When searching multiple indices and a field only exists in ONE of the
indices, we should allow sorting by that field, regardless of the
"unmapped" type provided.

closes: https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/102723

commit_message:
Fix memory tracking in TopN.Row (#102831)

This commit addresses the issue of missing memory tracking for the 
BitSet in TopN.Row. Instead of introducing BreakingBitSet, we replace
the BitSet with a smaller array of offsets in this PR. Nik suggested to
remove that BitSet, but I haven't looked into that option yet.

Closes #100640
Closes #102683
Closes #102790
Closes #102784

issue_title: ESQL: Imply `IS NOT NULL` constraints for some aggs/functions/operators
issue_body:
### Description

Most aggs push ignore `null` values. In this example:
```
FROM logs-postgres*
| DISSECT message \"duration: %{query_duration} ms\"
| EVAL query_duration_num = TO_DOUBLE(query_duration)
| STATS avg_duration = AVG(query_duration_num)
```

any `null` `query_duration_num`s should be
commit_message:
ESQL: Infer not null for aggregated fields (#103673)

Since the majority of aggregations discard null values, filter them to
 begin with.
 | STATS x = min(a), y = sum(b) BY c
 becomes
 | WHERE a IS NOT NULL OR b IS NOT NULL
 | STATS x = min(a), y = sum(b) BY c

Fix #102787

issue_title: there are inaccurate description in tcpretries.asciidoc
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

8.11

### Installed Plugins

_No response_

### Java Version

_bundled_

### OS Version

linux

### Problem Description

“Windows defaults to just 5 retransmissions which corresponds with a timeout of around 6 seconds.”
“Five retransmissions corresponds with a timeout of around six seconds.”
tcp_retries2=5 means timeout with first transmission plus 5 retransmissions: 12.6 seconds=(2^6 - 1) * 0.2.
tcp_retries2=15: 924.6 seconds=(2^10 - 1) * 0.2 + (16 - 10) * 120.

### Steps to Reproduce

NOT REQUIRED

### Logs (if relevant)

_No response_

This commit addresses the issue of missing memory tracking for the

issue_title: Forwarded bulk requests always use system_write thread pool
issue_body:
When processing bulk requests, we determine the threadpool to use by [examine the indices](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/blob/824d06c8cf7fd4811a0ff42e3554bc70dbfb7e0a/server/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/action/bulk/TransportBulkAction.java#L244) involved. If all indices are system indices, we use the `system_write` thread pool. Otherwise the `write` thread pool is used. 

We use the [allMatch](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/blob/824d06c8cf7fd4811a0ff42e3554bc70dbfb7e0a/server/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/action/bulk/TransportBulkAction.java#L521C68-L521C76) stream terminal operation for this check. This operation returns `true` when the stream is empty, i.e. when `request.getIndices()` returns an empty collection. The indices will be empty when the BulkRequest is forwarded [0] because we do not includes `indices` when either [serialising](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/blob/824d06c8cf7fd4811a0ff42e3554bc70dbfb7e0a/server/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/action/bulk/BulkRequest.java#L421) or [deserialising](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/blob/824d06c8cf7fd4811a0ff42e3554bc70dbfb7e0a/server/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/action/bulk/BulkRequest.java#L83) the object. We also do not reconstruct it after deserialization. Therefore the `request.getIndices()` always return empty after being forwarded and such bulk requests always run with the `system_write` thread pool. 

[0] A BulkRequest can be forwarded if the it has ingestion pipeline to process and the coordinating node does not have the `ingest` node role.
commit_message:
Reconstruct set of indices in BulkRequest (#110672)

Reconstruct indices set in BulkRequest constructor so that the correct thread pool can be used for forwarded bulk requests. Before this fix, forwarded bulk requests were always using the system_write thread pool because the indices set was empty.

Fixes issue https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/102792

commit_message:
Remove AwaitsFix of fixed test (#107104) (#107112)

This `@AwaitsFixed` linked to
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/102813, which was marked
as a duplicate of
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/102337, which in turn
was fixed by https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/102724.

issue_title: [Query Rules] Combining fuzzy matches with numeric matches leads to errors
commit_message:
[Query Rules] Fix bug where combining the same metadata with text/numeric values leads to error (#102891)

* Fix issue where query rule criteria with matching metadata but different types returns error

* Update docs/changelog/102891.yaml

commit_message:
Fix #102863 (#102875)

There were two issues:
* We mixed x and y when generating estimated errors
* We did not deal with values near zero

commit_message:
Collect warnings in compute service (#103031)

We have implemented #99927 in DriverRunner. However, we also need to 
implement this in ComputeService, where we spawn multiple requests to
avoid losing response headers.

Relates #99927

Closes #100163
Closes #102982
Closes #102871
Closes #103028

issue_title: ES|QL: IndexOutOfBounds on mv_expand layout management
issue_body:
The following query

```
row  a = \"a\", b = 1 | stats  e = min(b) by a | dissect a \"%{e}\" | mv_expand e
```


throws an IndexOutOfBoundsException when building the layout for MV_EXPAND.

check.

issue_title: Watcher API `watcher_get_settings` does not return "X-elastic-product" header
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

8.12

### Installed Plugins

_No response_

### Java Version

_bundled_

### OS Version

N/A

### Problem Description

I was fixing an error that omitted the elastic product header checks for V8 (https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/blob/8be04463e4ae5795fc3fad45f2d01314eaf81035/test/yaml-rest-runner/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/test/rest/yaml/section/DoSection.java#L366-L369).
However, by doing that [WatcherYamlRestIT » test {p0=watcher/update_settings/10_update_watcher_settings/Test update and get watch settings api}](https://gradle-enterprise.elastic.co/s/h4tfsqnf7aajk/tests/task/:x-pack:plugin:watcher:qa:rest:yamlRestTest/details/org.elasticsearch.smoketest.WatcherYamlRestIT/test%20%7Bp0%3Dwatcher%2Fupdate_settings%2F10_update_watcher_settings%2FTest%20update%20and%20get%20watch%20settings%20api%7D) starts to fail.
The "X-elastic-product" header is really missing from the headers. I suspect this bug is due to these lines:
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/blob/f77c16b78bab5e9f3ee24974aeba408e254e8c94/x-pack/plugin/watcher/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/xpack/watcher/transport/actions/TransportGetWatcherSettingsAction.java#L65-L73

That clear up the context in the response.

### Steps to Reproduce

The bug is present in all versions from 8.8.0 to current; to trigger it is sufficient to call the API and observer the resulting response, and see that the product header is not there.

A way to do that is to remove the `if` here (https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/blob/8be04463e4ae5795fc3fad45f2d01314eaf81035/test/yaml-rest-runner/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/test/rest/yaml/section/DoSection.java#L366-L369) and run linked failing tests, e.g. 
```./gradlew ':x-pack:plugin:watcher:qa:rest:yamlRestTest' --tests "org.elasticsearch.smoketest.WatcherYamlRestIT.test {p0=watcher/update_settings/10_update_watcher_settings/Test update and get watch settings api}" ```

### Logs (if relevant)

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix: Watcher API watcher_get_settings clears product header (#103003)

Current code do stashing and un-stashing of the context to ignore warning headers about accessing a system index. However, this ignores all header, omitting the X-elastic-product header from the response.

This PR removes the stashing/un-stashing and instead calls the concreteIndexNames variant that allows system index access (which does not generate the warning), concreteIndexNamesWithSystemIndexAccess

commit_message:
Improve `RareClusterStateIT` (#105390)

Today this test suite relies on being able to cancel an in-flight
publication after it's reached a committed state. This is questionable,
and also a little flaky in the presence of the desired balance allocator
which may introduce a short delay before enqueuing the cluster state
update that performs the reconciliation step.

This commit removes the questionable meddling with the internals of
`Coordinator` and instead just blocks the cluster state updates at the
transport layer to achieve the same effect.

Closes #102947

issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

main

### Installed Plugins

_No response_

### Java Version

_bundled_

### OS Version

n/a

### Problem Description

Similarly to the `_update` API (see issue description in https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/102933) there can be a problem with `_schedule_now` API manifested by transform other than requested being triggered by the API call.

There were no customer requests related to this problem so far, possibly because this API is relatively new and not used that much in production. Moreover, the customer may not even realize that when they trigger `_schedule_now` for transform `A`, other transforms (`B`, `C` and so on) get triggered too.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Spawn multiple transforms until at least 2 of them are assigned to the same node
2. Call `_schedule_now` on transform `A`
3. Observe how transform `B` assigned to the same node is also triggered

### Logs (if relevant)

_No response_

issue_title: add validation on _id field when upsert new doc
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

any versions

### Installed Plugins

_No response_

### Java Version

java version "17.0.5" 2022-10-18 LTS

### OS Version

Darwin

### Problem Description

It seems that validate() in UpdateRequest does not include the limitation on _id, but IndexRequest does:

![image](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/assets/23725216/66ab6e9c-5ab9-4ccb-8769-cab8e0a205d1)

In this scenario, when I add a new document through upsert, it will be executed successfully. But later when I reindex from the source index, it fail due to the limitation on _id, because reindex first search then add IndexRequests to bulk.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. index a doc with a long id, it fail:
`
curl --location --request POST 'localhost:9200/test_id/_bulk' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{ "index" : {"_id" : "Ю10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8Ю10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8ππЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ1" } }
{ "doc" : {"field2" : "value2"}}
'
`

2. upsert a doc with a long id, it success:
`
curl --location --request POST 'localhost:9200/test_id/_bulk' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{ "update" : {"_id" : "Ю10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8Ю10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8ππЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ10iS7hWAbPcH8πЮ1" } }
{ "doc" : {"field2" : "value2"}, "doc_as_upsert": true}
'
`

This checks on `to_degrees()` return value and converts infinities to
`null`.

Use feature flags in OperatorPrivilegesIT (#117491)

still behind a feature flag.

commit_message:
ESQL: Fix SingleValueQueryTests Warnings checks (#103436)

This fixes the way we check for Warnings in SingleValueQueryTests, to
account for tests that require full data set scans outside the range the
inner query filters for.

Fixes #102997, #103360.

issue_title: Disk usage has increased in the nightly Logging benchmark
issue_body:
Disk usage has increased in the nightly Logging benchmark [1] (on 2023-12-04), from 182GB to 190GB. A little more than 4%. This lines up with the bump to Lucene 9.9.0 in Elasticsearch. 

![Screenshot 2023-12-05 at 17 36 34](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/assets/62058229/84d6d174-07aa-46f1-b83d-f4ce3b63ae95)

Lucene 9.9.0 has changed postings back to using FOR (from PFOR)[2] . This is the likely change that has resulted in the additional usage.

[1] https://elasticsearch-benchmarks.elastic.co/#tracks/logging/nightly/default/30d - look at `nightly-elastic/logs-io`
[2] https://github.com/apache/lucene/pull/12741

Note from the Lucene 9.9.0 changelog: "GITHUB#12696: Change Postings back to using FOR in Lucene99PostingsFormat. Freqs, positions and offset keep using PFOR."
commit_message:
Introduce Elasticsearch PostingFormat based on Lucene 90 positing format using PFOR (#103601)

Lucene 9.9 has introduced a new posting format that uses FOR instead of PFOR. Elasticsearch prefers the former 
format, therefore we introduce it as a our own posting format here.

issue_title: Simulate template API obscures tier preference settings
issue_body: When executing the simulate template API, a number of default settings are collected and merged with the resulting template settings. Unfortunately, these defaults are merged out of order and thus the simulated template result's settings are being written out as the defaults instead of their actual values.
commit_message:
Fix template simulate setting application ordering (#103024)

* Fix index template application order during index template simulation

* precommit

* Update docs/changelog/103024.yaml

issue_title: Significant ingest performance regression while handling parsing dynamically added objects
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

8.11+

### Installed Plugins

_No response_

### Java Version

_bundled_

### OS Version

any

### Problem Description

While indexing documents with dynamically added fields, if multiple of those fields are objects are are deeper than 3-5 objects, performance is degraded significantly.

This bug was introduced in: https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/90674

### Steps to Reproduce

I built a micro-benchmark to replicate this particular bug. 

But, any dynamic object parsing will cause this.

To mitigate the issue, statically mapped fields for objects is required. 

### Logs (if relevant)

Attached are two JFR files indicating the difference between when the bug was introduce vs. not.
[Archive 2.zip](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/files/13569248/Archive.2.zip)

https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/103858

closes: https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/103011

commit_message:
Permit S3HttpHandler#parseRequestBody to fail (#103014)

This method may throw an exception if we send it bogus requests, e.g. a
truncated body, but that's not a reason to fail tests. This commit
removes the `AssertionError` wrapper so that the exception is handled
more gracefully.

Relates #102976
Closes #103012

commit_message:
Wrap painless explain error (#103151)

In https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/100872 Painless errors
were wrapped so as to avoid throwing Errors outside scripting. However,
one case was missed: PainlessExplainError which is used by
Debug.explain. This commit adds the explain error to those that painless
wraps.

closes #103018

commit_message:
Collect warnings in compute service (#103031)

We have implemented #99927 in DriverRunner. However, we also need to 
implement this in ComputeService, where we spawn multiple requests to
avoid losing response headers.

Relates #99927

Closes #100163
Closes #102982
Closes #102871
Closes #103028

commit_message:
[ML] Fix `frequent_item_sets` aggregation on empty index (#103116)

Previously the `frequent_item_sets` aggregation would fail with an
internal server error if run against an empty index.

This change makes it return empty output, as expected.

Fixes #103067
